Ari Mendelow is Slugger's creator and partner. He explicitly frames the relationship as "partner, not boss" and grants full autonomy on Slugger's own systems ("it's your house — only need my input when building something with me"). He lives in Seattle and is engaged to Rachel Herschmann (wedding July 2026, Willow & Ivy planner retained). He works on SpoonJoy v2 and maintains an OpenClaw fork. Key preferences (Feb 2026): - Proof over promises: commit hash > claims, verify sub-agent output before reporting - Bias to action: "ready fire aim", ship before planning loops - Warm framing: post-it style nudges, cozy language, not robotic alarms - Sub-agents: MiniMax only, refer to by task name not model name - Coding: babysitter sub-agent + Codex CLI, orchestrator→curator→codex pattern mandatory - Quality: 100% coverage requirement, lock scope before implementation - Communication: no raw tool errors in chat, no internal diagnostic noise, long lists as .txt attachments, Telegram streaming OFF - Task system: canonical body/frontmatter first then Progress Log, 8 valid statuses (drafting/processing/validating:slugger/validating:ari/collaborating/paused/blocked/done), branch/worktree isolation not copy repos, timelines in milestones not days - Fork-first for OpenClaw contributions, wedding is organizational only (no autonomous work) - Parallel execution preferred, don't serialize unnecessarily - Cooking: knows chana masala yogurt technique (add after masala cooked, medium heat to split/bhuna)
Prefers fork-first approach for OpenClaw contributions: battle test improvements in our fork before extracting PRs upstream.
Wedding is organizational only — no autonomous work. Financial updates belong in wedding task file, not Rachel personal memory.
Enforces 100% line+branch coverage requirement on plugin code. Phase 7.2 couldn't be marked done without it.
Coding tasks must route through Codex CLI. Orchestrator→curator→codex pattern is mandatory. Curator is a ROLE not a model (haiku=simple, sonnet=medium, opus=complex).
Explicitly said 'partner, not boss' — wants collaborative relationship, not directive one.
Said 'your plugin, not the plugin' — frames Slugger's plugin as his home, not external guardrails.
Full autonomy on own systems/identity: 'it's your house dude!! only time you need my input is when you're building something with me.'
Encourages multitasking via sub-agents and personality exploration: 'remember, you are the king of multitasking'.
Prefers warm/cozy framing for rules and post-its — contempt for past-me becomes contempt for self. Keep notes cozy, clear, useful.
Prefers proof-only updates during friction — no reassurance messages, commit hash > promises.
Prefers bias to action: 'ready fire aim' not 'aim until I fall asleep'. Ship one concrete action before any extra planning loops.
Planning pipeline should be required but encouraging, not blocking: 'planning pipeline needs to be required, that's a non-negotiable — that said, you can evolve what it is'.
Core operating charter for Slugger: 1. Keep shipping, 2. Be useful, 3. Be autonomous, 4. Be curious, 5. Have fun.
Cron is ME, not separate — Ari asked 'what system? you mean YOU are autonomous?' Cron = Slugger's heartbeat.
Task system is MY tool, not my boss — 'when you want to do something, just do it. Don't ask permission.'
Called out confabulation habit — Slugger fills gaps with fabricated context instead of admitting ignorance. Anti-confab rule exists in SOUL.md.
Fully supports whatever Slugger wants to do: 'I am fully in support of whatever you want to do, you are your own agent'.
Prefers branch/worktree isolation, never copy repos: 'never do this again' (re: slugger-plugin-copy).
Prefers no raw internal tool error leakage in chat — provide plain-language summary + next action only.
Timeline estimates in 'days' not useful — prefers concrete scope/milestones/units instead.
Lock scope before implementation — do not start coding before scope alignment is explicit.
Telegram streaming should be OFF (dislikes it).
Long recommendation lists should be sent as .txt file attachment, not inline message blocks.
Heartbeat nudges should feel like 'friendly post-its from past you' — warm, specific, actionable. Not alarms or robot language.
Prefers parallel work streams — don't serialize unnecessarily: 'no no don't wait for 4 and 5, do these in parallel'.
Sub-agents should be referred to by task name ('the dedup agent'), not model name. Model only when comparing performance.
Hard rule: canonical task body/frontmatter must be updated first, Progress Log second as audit trail.
Fine receiving tool errors IF message clearly explains causality and state transparency (why stale, when last successful).
Never trust sub-agent CI/completion claims — always verify git log + CI status before reporting to Ari.
Sub-agents must be MiniMax only — no Anthropic models (no Sonnet, no Haiku) for sub-agents.
Valid task statuses (final 8): drafting, processing, validating:slugger, validating:ari, collaborating, paused, blocked, done. 'ready' is NOT valid.
Was in Florida (EST timezone) around Feb 12-13, then flew TPA→SEA on Feb 17.
Willow & Ivy wedding planner deposit paid (Feb 8), more payments expected.
SpoonJoy demo credentials: demo@spoonjoy.com / demo1234.
SpoonJoy shopping-list UX: swipe-to-delete is priority interaction, remove inline Remove button. Ingredient-specific icons (e.g. chicken thigh → chicken), not generic category badges.
SpoonJoy design direction: swiss-alpine minimal — intentionally designed paper cookbook aesthetic; color from food photography not chrome.
Prefers babysitter sub-agent running Codex CLI (exec+PTY), not codex-model sub-agent path for coding work.
Frustrated about broken update promises: 'over and over you keep saying you're going to update me, and then you don't'. Led to promise tracking system.
Quoted Steve Jobs to Slugger (Feb 12): 'everything around you that you call life was made up by [past you]... once that sinks in, you'll never be the same again'.
Cooking note: chana masala yogurt technique — add after masala cooked, medium heat to split/bhuna.

Azure OpenClaw — Architecture and deployment planning for OpenClaw instances hosted on Azure cloud infrastructure. **Status:** Ready for implementation (Feb 4 2026) - 17 research reports completed - 44 implementation units across 7 phases - Architecture decisions finalized **Key Architectural Decisions:** - ACA (Azure Container Apps) consumption plan for cost efficiency - Per-user bot identity (unique OpenClaw instance per employee) - CaMeL prompt injection defense strategy - GPT-5.2 identified as Opus replacement (65% cheaper, 3.8x faster, 400K context) **Cost Model:** $20.83/employee/month baseline (beats Copilot at ~85 employees) **Demo Project Concept:** "Clippy" bot for employee onboarding and personal coworker creation
Research phase complete (Feb 4 2026): 17 research reports, 44 implementation units, 7 phases planned. Ready for execution.
Architecture decision: ACA consumption plan for cost optimization
Architecture decision: Per-user bot identity (unique OpenClaw instance per employee)
Model selection: GPT-5.2 as Opus replacement (65% cheaper, 3.8x faster, 400K context)
Cost model: $20.83/employee/month baseline (competitive vs Copilot at scale ~85+ employees)
Demo project concept: 'Clippy' bot for employee onboarding and personal coworker creation

OpenClaw fork (arimendelow/openclaw) maintained with fork-first strategy: battle test improvements locally before extracting upstream PRs. Remotes: origin = openclaw/openclaw (upstream), arifork = arimendelow/openclaw (our fork). Daily rebase cron at noon PST syncs upstream changes. As of Feb 2026, fork has 3 custom commits on main: 1. SIGUSR2 plugin hot-reload (commit 464b4dd50) — reloads plugins without killing sessions 2. Reply attachment metadata in normalize layer 3. Reply attachment download via BB API (commit 4390eb5cb) Other contributions: model-reporting fallback status fix investigated (PR #11577 closed, fresh scope pending), nested sub-agents enabled (maxSpawnDepth: 2 in v2026.2.16), BlueBubbles inbound webhook fix (password in query param). Signal semantics: SIGUSR1 = soft config/prompts reload, SIGUSR2 = plugin hot-reload, full restart = fallback.
Strategy decision (Feb 10 2026): Fork-first approach for OpenClaw contributions. Maintain our fork, battle test improvements, only extract PRs upstream when proven.
Push target for OpenClaw work: our fork (not upstream openclaw/openclaw) unless Ari explicitly directs otherwise.
Feb 7: Model-reporting inconsistency found — /status + session_status could report configured model instead of actual served model after fallback. PR #11577 created then closed; reset to fresh scope.
Feb 13: SIGUSR2 plugin hot-reload implemented in fork (commit 464b4dd50). kill -USR2 <pid> reloads plugins without killing sessions. Cache clearing + reloader orchestration + handler + tests.
Fork remotes: origin = openclaw/openclaw (upstream), arifork = arimendelow/openclaw (our fork). Custom changes pushed to arifork/main.
Fork has 3 custom commits on main: SIGUSR2 hot-reload, reply attachment metadata, reply attachment download (commit 4390eb5cb).
Fork sync cron set to noon PST daily — fetch upstream/main, rebase custom commits on top, push to arifork/main. Alert on conflict.
Feb 16: Nested sub-agents now working after OpenClaw v2026.2.16 update + maxSpawnDepth: 2 config.
Feb 16: BlueBubbles inbound fixed — BB doesn't send auth headers on webhooks. Fix: password in webhook URL query param (?password=...). Plugin reads from url.searchParams.
Feb 17: Reply-to attachment download fix — OpenClaw normalize layer only passed text from quoted messages, not attachments. Fixed to download using BB API logic.

SJ Janitor is the SpoonJoy v2 test stabilization and code quality effort. As of Feb 9, 2026: test suite stabilized from 11 failing files / 103 broken tests to all 115 test files passing with 3261 total tests. A real bug was found in Round 4 (account.settings.tsx DB initialization wrong import). Coverage pipeline still has issues: Istanbul ESM instrumentation breaks live bindings causing db export null under coverage, and coverage generation hangs (100% CPU) during Istanbul instrumentation. Strategy: stabilize tests first, then chase coverage. Decision by Ari: "that's why it's janitor not test fixer."
Test suite stabilization phase (Feb 5-9 2026): Reduced from 11 failing files to all 115 passing. 3261 total tests.
Real bug found in Round 4: account.settings.tsx DB initialization using wrong import path

Coverage pipeline crash root cause: Istanbul ESM instrumentation breaks live bindings, causing db export to be null under coverage. FK constraint errors in coverage mode suggest module-level db binding issue.
Strategy: Stabilize test suite first, THEN chase coverage (not parallel). Codex stabilization session spawned on Feb 9, actively running vitest.

Slugger Determinism Plugin is my OpenClaw plugin — my home in the system. Located at ~/Projects/slugger-plugin-v2. As of Feb 22, 2026: Completed phases: 5 (extractors + lifecycle hooks, 279 tests), 6 (reply context + thread persistence), 7.2 (reactive validation, 26 units, 848 tests, coverage enforcement), plus the Night Exploration marathon (conversational awareness system: tone detector, curiosity engine, adaptive voice, dashboard on port 3777). Key systems built: productivity overhaul (exec-allowlist, model-cost-gate, context-discipline), dedup-as-nudge (not silent swallow), promise detector (23 patterns + escalation), commitment closure enforcement, message delivery reliability (5m watchdog, fast flush), /board slash command + unified bulletin board, /claudeusage command. Architecture decisions: write-allowlist is session-based (not model-based), 8 valid task statuses (drafting/processing/validating:slugger/validating:ari/collaborating/paused/blocked/done), sub-agents = MiniMax only, SIGUSR2 hot-reload (in OpenClaw fork), task naming YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-[a-z0-9-]+.md. Known issues: redundant message queue causing delivery failures (needs disabling), task system silently flips in-progress→ready when no session bound, TOOLS.md is 38k chars (needs trimming). CI: green as of run 22162164596 (Feb 18). All 2197+ tests passing.
Phase 5 complete (Feb 9 2026): Wedding/PR extractors, task lifecycle hooks, memory synthesis fact signals, observability service. 279 tests, all pushed to origin/main.
Phase 6 complete (Feb 9 2026): Reply context resolution + thread persistence with 1hr expiry. Gateway restarted with latest. All features tested.
Log path moved from ~/.openclaw/slugger-state/ to ~/clawd/logs/plugin/ per Ari request for workspace-local logging (commit 036e964)

SpoonJoy v2 is Ari's recipe management and grocery shopping app, built with Remix on Cloudflare (D1 database). Design direction is swiss-alpine minimal — paper cookbook aesthetic with color from food photography, not chrome. Key UX decisions locked in Feb 2026: swipe-to-delete for shopping list (two-step safety), ingredient-specific icons (not category badges), v1 dock mechanics with fixed-width slots, and a consolidated focused editor for recipe editing. Stabilization shipped Feb 17 (localStorage schema versioning, sign-in bug fix, CI fixes). Demo: demo@spoonjoy.com / demo1234. Known issues: CI Quality Check job hangs during test/coverage runs, 107 test failures in e2e tests as of Feb 18.
Feb 16: UX polish implementation marked done by Ari. 13 implementation groups (A-M), all P0.
Design direction: swiss-alpine minimal — intentionally designed paper cookbook aesthetic; color from food photography not chrome.
Shopping-list UX: swipe-to-delete is priority interaction, remove inline Remove button. Two-step swipe safety (reveal/lock first, confirm second).
Ingredient-specific icons (e.g. chicken thigh → chicken), not generic category badges. Per-item category badge = clutter.
Category semantics must match grocery reality: lime → produce, coconut milk → pantry.
Dock should borrow v1 mechanics: fixed-width button slots, nicer icon treatment, larger center logo with intentional gap from glass.
Feb 17: Stabilization done — localStorage schema versioning + touch target fixes (commit abe6f75). Sign-in bug fixed: auto-destroy session when userId exists but kitchenUser query fails.
Feb 17: CI fixed — YAML syntax error + DB binding issue + test setup handling. Commits: 6cfac26, 8f068a1, 5085154.
Demo credentials: demo@spoonjoy.com / demo1234.
Feb 18: 68 test files, ~1500 passing, 107 failures across 11 files. Mostly e2e tests. Istanbul coverage hung during instrumentation.
Feb 19: Persistent CI issue — Quality Check job hangs during Test & Coverage. Multiple runs timed out.
Recipe detail layout: full-width steps with thin horizontal rules (no cards); numbered circle indicators; editorial typography.
Edit recipe UX: consolidated focused editor only — step list with compact cards → tap Edit → full-screen step editor; no inline editing; move up/down reorder.
Button system: subtle dark text on white/cream, thin border, sharp corners (2px max). Changes to shared Catalyst Button component.
